mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or t-test p-value exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in real sales and housing starts indicates broad-based aggregate demand recovery, driving positive quarterly equity returns through earnings growth.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with Rank IC below 0.01 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side bottlenecks and profit margin compression, leading to negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods show positive forward returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or correlation coefficient exceeds 0.0.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL exceeding 330.0 triggers inflation fears and expectations of aggressive central bank policy, prompting immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 330.0 yield positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising payroll employment and falling unemployment rate indicates labor market tightness, supporting positive quarterly equity returns through wage-driven consumption.
failure_condition: Divergence pattern fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with Rank IC below 0.01 over a 5-year rolling window or t-test p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly GDP growth reflects strong aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical correlation with 20-day forward returns, with p-value exceeding 0.10 over the full sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 2017 baseline index of 100.0 signals severe supply contraction, triggering immediate negative equity returns due to earnings downgrade expectations.
failure_condition: INDPRO values below 100.0 produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.
